svg.text: User-managed OAuth app. Every customer connects their own Zoom account.
All traffic is HTTPS. OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM in Neon Postgres.
app.qualitative.one · hosted on Vercel Fluid Compute (us-east)
Crash-safe, retries on transient error, no Zoom calls (works on cached transcript)
Key in env, never in DB
All Zoom tokens are encrypted before insert; decrypted only at the data-access boundary.
Signs each delivery with HMAC-SHA256 over body
Neon Postgres (managed by Neon, AWS us-east)
TLS in transit, AES-256 at rest (Neon-managed disk)
• extracts (AI-generated insights tied to a meeting)
Strict tenant isolation: every query carries account_id at the type level.
• Commercial terms — does not train on customer data
No Zoom credentials ever leave Qualitative. Gemini sees plain
transcript text and no Zoom IDs/URLs/tokens.
• Stripe — billing only; receives subscription email, no Zoom data
• Vercel Analytics — anonymous pageviews / product events
• Google OAuth — app sign-in (separate flow from Zoom OAuth)
These never receive Zoom user data, tokens, or recording content.
2. User OAuth connection flow (one time per user)
User approves the 6 scopes on Zoom’s page
Server reads encrypted tokens from Neon & decrypts in memory.
If access token expired: POST zoom.us/oauth/token → new pair, re-encrypt & store.
For each meeting: download VTT transcript from the file’s download URL.
Persist meeting + transcript + attendees to Neon (account-scoped).
UI refreshes; user may click “Process & Extract” (no further Zoom calls).
All Zoom requests use Authorization: Bearer <decrypted access token>.
Tokens are never logged. Decrypted plaintext exists only in request memory.
URL validation (first-time only): respond with HMAC(plainToken, secret).
Reject if timestamp older than 5 min (replay defense).
Look up local user by payload.object.host_email (case-insensitive).
Re-fetch that user’s recent recordings (transcript URL is most reliable from API).
Sync the meeting + transcript into Neon (idempotent on external_id).
200 OK to Zoom (always, even on internal error — avoids retry storms).
Endpoint is public (Zoom doesn’t authenticate as a user). Signature check is the only auth.
Disconnected users: tokens cleared and webhook event silently dropped.
Privacy policy: https://qualitative.one/privacy · Terms: https://qualitative.one/terms · DPA: https://qualitative.one/dpa
